title: podman stop <already-stopped-container> : pass or fail?
body:
What should `podman stop` do when run on an already-stopped container? Currently it fails:
```
# podman run --name foo fedora date
Mon Apr  2 15:10:09 UTC 2018
# podman stop foo
container_linux.go:393: signaling init process caused "os: process already finished"
failed to stop container 6b9089323168acd6b11bbed10b8c42db9bd4e06bdb2fc9123f140dac64f7e3bd: error sending signal to container 6b9089323168acd6b11bbed10b8c42db9bd4e06bdb2fc9123f140dac64f7e3bd: `/usr/bin/runc kill 6b9089323168acd6b11bbed10b8c42db9bd4e06bdb2fc9123f140dac64f7e3bd 15` failed: exit status 1
# echo $?
125                        <--- presumably because #412 was an incomplete fix

```
docker echoes/confirms the argument (container name or id) and exits immediately with status 0.

podman-0.3.5-1.gitdb6bf9e.fc27.x86_64

comments.body: We should follow the docker standard.
I think that our API call returning an error that the container isn't running is fine. `podman stop` should catch the error, and if the container is already stopped, return cleanly.
